flashcards.back: A collection of distinct objects, considered as an object in its own right.
The set containing no elements, denoted by ∅ or {}.
A is a subset of B - every element of A is also an element of B.
The union of A and B - set of all elements in A or B or both.
The intersection of A and B - set of all elements in both A and B.
Set difference - elements in A but not in B.
The set of all subsets of a given set.

flashcards.front: What is the cardinality of a set?
flashcards.back: The number of elements in the set, denoted |A|.

flashcards.front: What is De Morgan's Law for sets?
flashcards.back: (A ∪ B)ᶜ = Aᶜ ∩ Bᶜ and (A ∩ B)ᶜ = Aᶜ ∪ Bᶜ

flashcards.front: What is the Cartesian product A × B?
flashcards.back: The set of all ordered pairs (a, b) where a ∈ A and b ∈ B.
A diagram showing all possible logical relations between a finite collection of sets.
A ⊂ B if A ⊆ B but A ≠ B.

flashcards.front: What is symmetric difference A Δ B?
flashcards.back: Elements in A or B but not in both: (A - B) ∪ (B - A)
A notation that describes a set by specifying properties its members must satisfy.
